title: Memorandum Circular No. 1250, s. 1982
text:
Office of the President of the Philippines Malacañang

MEMORANDUM CIRCULAR NO. 1250

AUTHORIZING ATTENDANCE IN THE NATIONAL CONFERENCE AND ECHO CONFERENCE ON PUBLIC ADMINISTRATION

The College of Public Administration, University of the Philippines, its Alumni Association, the Philippine Society for Public Administration and the Association of Schools of Public Administration in the Philippines will jointly hold a National Conference on Public Administration in Manila on November 30 to December 3, 1982 and an Echo Conference in the regions some time in 1983.

The Conference aims to provide an opportunity for the professional discussions of the problems and prospects of public administration and to share experiences in the practice of public administration.

All government officials and faculty members of academic institutions who hold degree/degrees in Public Administration/Public Management or Educational Administration/Management in the graduate level, and/or graduates of: Career Executive Service Development (CESD), Philippine Executive Academy (PEA), Local Administration Development Program (LADP), and National Defense College of the Philippines (NDCP) are authorized to attend the conference on official time with reasonable expenses for transportation, per diems and a conference fee of ₱500.00 per participant, chargeable against the appropriations of their respective offices, in accordance with the provisions of Presidential Decree No. 344 dated November 26, 1973, as implemented by COA General Circular No. 127, series of 1973, subject to the availability of funds and the usual accounting and auditing requirements, as an exception to Letter of Instructions No. 1098 dated January 5, 1981.

It is understood that the participants residing or stationed within the Greater Manila Area, as well as those within the regions with reference to the Echo Conference, shall be entitled to reasonable expenses for transportation and conference fees only.

The participants shall submit to their respective office heads a report on their attendance in the conference within thirty (30) days after the termination thereof. Likewise, within the same period, the organizers shall submit to this Office a report and evaluation of the conference, copy furnished the Budget Commission including a list of participants and observers indicating their respective offices. The report thereon shall include a financial statement of income and expenses duly audited by a Certified Public Accountant not connected with the organizers.

By authority of the President:

(Sgd.) JUAN C. TUVERA Presidential Executive Assistant

Manila, October 29, 1982

Source: Presidential Management Staff
